jokes.text: I was making love to a beautiful woman... and her boyfriend, and a third person whose name I never learned
jokes.setup: Standard political apology about infidelity
jokes.punchline: Escalates to threesome then foursome with unnamed participant
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure - each revelation gets more absurd. The casual mention of not learning the third person's name is brilliantly dismissive.

jokes.text: Furthermore, it was wrong of me to say I was building houses for the underprivileged when I was actually having four-way sex in a cave in Brazil
jokes.setup: Another political lie about charitable work
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was cover for cave orgy in Brazil
jokes.explanation: The contrast between noble charity work and cave sex in Brazil is perfectly absurd. Sets up the cave sex discussion.

jokes.text: I bet cave sex is in-sane. - Why? - The echoes and the humidity.
jokes.setup: Speculation about cave sex being intense
jokes.punchline: Practical considerations of acoustics and moisture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan delivery treating cave sex logistics as a practical engineering problem. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: In my defense, it was my birthday, and I really wanted to do it
jokes.setup: Preparing for another excuse for scandalous behavior
jokes.punchline: Childishly simple justification for cave orgy
jokes.explanation: Character-driven humor showing Dexhart's childish reasoning. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: not whether he was high on nitrous and cocaine during the cave sex... which, by the way, I heard he was
jokes.setup: Claims to only care about policies, not scandal details
jokes.punchline: Immediately shares more scandalous details
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - trying to be high-minded while gossiping. The 'by the way' delivery is excellent.

jokes.text: councilman Dexhart may have also had sex with a prostitute in the limousine on the way to and from the press conference where he apologized for having an affair
jokes.setup: News about Dexhart's continued scandals
jokes.punchline: Had sex during the trip to apologize for having sex
jokes.explanation: The recursive nature of the scandal - having sex while going to apologize for sex - is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: You're married, and you hit on women constantly. Yeah, but I've never sealed the deal. Just window shopping.
jokes.setup: Accusation of improper behavior while married
jokes.punchline: Tom's shopping metaphor for infidelity
jokes.explanation: Good Tom character moment with the retail metaphor, though not particularly original.

jokes.text: You can fly to Brazil, just don't enter the cave. Up top.
jokes.setup: Continuing the travel/shopping metaphor
jokes.punchline: References the cave sex scandal as metaphor for crossing the line
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that works the cave reference into Tom's metaphor. The 'Up top' request makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I changed my name, which is legal, and once in high school, a girl beat me in a wrestling match. That's it.
jokes.setup: Claiming to be squeaky clean
jokes.punchline: His 'scandals' are pathetically innocent
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for Ben as boringly wholesome. The wrestling loss adds vulnerability.

jokes.text: You will never believe what I just found on Jerry's Facebook. A friend. Burn.
jokes.setup: Building suspense about scandalous Facebook discovery
jokes.punchline: Jerry having even one friend is noteworthy
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and misdirection. Jerry's social isolation as the 'scandal' is mean but funny. Great economy.

jokes.text: He's probably gonna go anger pee in the courtyard.
jokes.setup: Jerry leaving upset about the game
jokes.punchline: Prediction of his specific coping mechanism
jokes.explanation: Good character observation about Jerry's patterns. 'Anger pee' is a funny specific behavior.

jokes.text: Roger that. - Copy. Over and out.
jokes.setup: Simple confirmation of a date
jokes.punchline: Both use overly formal/military communication
jokes.explanation: Mild awkwardness showing both characters being overly formal about dating. Decent character work.

jokes.text: That was my phone.
jokes.setup: Leslie appears nervous talking about her date
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly explaining why she might seem flustered
jokes.explanation: Minor awkward moment showing Leslie's nervousness. Not particularly funny but serves character.

jokes.text: My taxes pay your salary, right? Cool.
jokes.setup: Learning about Ron's dirt-digging activities
jokes.punchline: Casual approval of wasting taxpayer time
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reaction to government inefficiency. The casual 'Cool' is funny.

jokes.text: Like, I don't know, pair of cargo pants. I wouldn't go with a cargo pant. What about, like, a sexy hat? I don't know what that even is.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking for first date outfit advice
jokes.punchline: Suggests cargo pants and 'sexy hat' - undefined items
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie fashion cluelessness. 'Sexy hat' as undefined concept is funny, Ann's bewilderment works.

jokes.text: Sure, yeah. Tonight? Several hours from now? Or you could just come over now? I think that would better. Let's go.
jokes.setup: Ann offering to help Leslie later
jokes.punchline: Leslie's impatient escalation to immediate help
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's impatience and anxiety well. The quick escalation is in character but not highly funny.

jokes.text: This isn't even a real receipt, just a scrap of paper. Taliban robes!
jokes.setup: Defending a fake receipt as evidence
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivots to much more damaging photo evidence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from petty evidence to serious accusation. The sudden topic change works.

jokes.text: Those aren't taliban robes. That's from Halloween. I was dressed like a jedi. I'm sure that the voters would be able to tell the difference.
jokes.setup: Defending himself against Taliban accusation
jokes.punchline: Naive faith in voters' ability to distinguish context
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ben's political naivety. The sarcastic confidence is funny.

jokes.text: Looks to me like you're in the taliban.
jokes.setup: Ben's reasonable explanation about Halloween costume
jokes.punchline: Tom's flat dismissal of context
jokes.explanation: Tom's stubborn refusal to accept context is in character. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: That's right. Ron has two ex-wives, each named Tammy. Both of them bitches. Yes, my mom's name is Tammy.
jokes.setup: Revealing Ron's complicated relationship history
jokes.punchline: Ron calling his own mother a bitch along with ex-wives
jokes.explanation: Excellent character revelation about Ron. The escalation to including his mother is perfectly Ron. Great economy and impact.

jokes.text: Tom! That was a jaeger-secret. You just breached a jaeger-secret. Damn! This just heated up quick.
jokes.setup: Tom revealing Ron's personal information
jokes.punchline: Ron's serious reaction using made-up military-style terminology
jokes.explanation: Great Ron character moment with 'jaeger-secret' - sounds official but made-up. Tom's excited reaction adds to it.

jokes.text: Will you be wearing it out of the store today, madame? I think I will, good lady.
jokes.setup: Standard retail interaction about wearing purchase
jokes.punchline: Both playing up formal retail politeness
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment showing their friendship dynamic. Not particularly funny but pleasant.

jokes.text: if I've been married? Have you? No. Then say that. But he'll wonder why I haven't been. You know what? I'm gonna say that I was married, but the question is, should I say that I have kids?
jokes.setup: Simple question about marital status
jokes.punchline: Leslie's spiral into creating fake backstory
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Leslie's tendency to overthink and complicate simple situations.

jokes.text: What if he shows up with another woman? What if one of my sleeves catches on fire and it spreads rapidly? What if, instead of tic tacs, I accidentally pop a couple of ambien, and I have to keep punching my leg to stay awake?
jokes.setup: Leslie's dating anxieties
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and specific catastrophic scenarios
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Leslie's anxiety into absurd specificity. The Ambien/leg-punching scenario is brilliantly weird.

jokes.text: Those are all insane hypotheticals, and I promise you they won't happen. They have happened. All of these have happened to me.
jokes.setup: Ann reassuring Leslie these scenarios are impossible
jokes.punchline: Leslie reveals they're all from personal experience
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - what seemed like neurotic overthinking becomes established dating history. Perfect structure.

jokes.text: One time, I accidentally drank an entire bottle of vinegar. I thought it was terrible wine.
jokes.setup: Starting to list her actual dating disasters
jokes.punchline: Mistaking vinegar for wine and drinking the whole bottle
jokes.explanation: Good absurd Leslie moment. The idea of drinking a whole bottle of vinegar thinking it's bad wine is funny.

jokes.text: One time, I rode in a sidecar on a guy's motorcycle, and the sidecar detached and went down a flight of stairs.
jokes.setup: Another dating disaster story
jokes.punchline: Sidecar detaching and rolling down stairs
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy scenario. The specificity of stairs makes it funnier than just 'detached'.

jokes.text: I literally woke up with his hand in my mouth. We went out after that, but then he got weird.
jokes.setup: Man trying to pull out her tooth while she slept
jokes.punchline: They continued dating, but HE got weird
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leslie logic - tooth extraction during sleep is fine, but he became the problem later. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: I know what you're thinking. I wear an earpiece, you sit at a table nearby, you speak into a mike, you tell me what to say on the date, but let me tell you, it never works.
jokes.setup: Ann suggesting practice dates
jokes.punchline: Leslie assumes spy movie scenario and reveals it's failed before
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie character moment showing her overthinking and past dating preparation attempts.

jokes.text: Whales, parades, electricity... And the rest are blank. I couldn't think of anything else.
jokes.setup: Leslie prepared conversation topics
jokes.punchline: Only managed three random topics, gave up
jokes.explanation: The random nature of the three topics and admitting defeat is very Leslie. Good character work.

jokes.text: Is she practice laughing?
jokes.setup: Leslie preparing for her practice date
jokes.punchline: Ann observing Leslie rehearsing laughter
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Leslie's over-preparation. Visual gag of rehearsed laughter is funny.

jokes.text: The Danish call it oppe og nede apparat, which literally translated means 'the up and down machine'. That's a thorough history of the teeter-totter.
jokes.setup: Leslie practicing conversation
jokes.punchline: Exhaustive explanation of playground equipment with fake foreign language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie over-preparation. The made-up Danish and technical approach to teeter-totters is in character.

jokes.text: How big is it? Really? Oh, my God.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to ask a getting-to-know-you question
jokes.punchline: Accidentally asks sexually suggestive question
jokes.explanation: Standard accidental innuendo joke. Works for showing Leslie's nervousness but not very original.

jokes.text: Do you hate black people? Excuse me? 'Cause apparently in 1988, you donated money to the presidential campaign for... David Duke! The KKK guy?
jokes.setup: Tom confronting Donna with research
jokes.punchline: Reveals shockingly inappropriate political donation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and misdirection. The blunt question followed by the reveal works well.

jokes.text: I got a phone call. They said he would lower taxes.
jokes.setup: Explaining donation to KKK leader
jokes.punchline: Purely practical tax-based reasoning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna deadpan response. The casual, practical approach to a horrific political choice is darkly funny.

jokes.text: We're celebrating, 'cause she's super rich and super hot also. Okay, come on, Tommy. Cut it out. You're super hot. Everybody else has to deal with it.
jokes.setup: Tom bragging about his wife
jokes.punchline: Continuing to objectify her despite her obvious discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good Tom character moment showing his inappropriate behavior. The cringe is intentional and effective.

jokes.text: I've established a scientifically perfect ten-point scale of human beauty. Wendy is a 7.4, which is way too high for Tom, who is a 3.8. Ten is tennis legend Steffi Graf.
jokes.setup: Ron commenting on Tom's marriage
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-scientific rating system with Steffi Graf as perfection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron deadpan delivery with absurd specificity. Steffi Graf as the 10 is randomly perfect. Great character work.

jokes.text: You're late, and I can see your nipples through your dress. What? No. Really?
jokes.setup: Ann playing a terrible date for practice
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate comment about Leslie's appearance
jokes.explanation: Good cringe practice date moment. Ann's commitment to being terrible is funny.

jokes.text: What's your niece's name? Torple. What? I don't know. That's not a name. I don't have a niece. My niece's name is Stephanie? Stop lying!
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to explain being late with fake story
jokes.punchline: Makes up ridiculous name, gets caught, keeps changing story
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Leslie's bad lying. 'Torple' is a perfectly ridiculous made-up name.

jokes.text: I have to go to the... whizz palace. You know, Dave, the... the place where you... you know, the toilet thing. It's a bathroom! It's called a bathroom!
jokes.setup: Leslie needing to excuse herself politely
jokes.punchline: Can't remember the word 'bathroom', creates increasingly awkward descriptions
jokes.explanation: 'Whizz palace' is funny, and the escalation to Ann's exasperation works well. Good Leslie panic moment.

jokes.text: I didn't know I was adopted.
jokes.setup: Tom using Jerry's adoption as dirt
jokes.punchline: Jerry learning about his adoption through the game
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's genuinely uncomfortable. Jerry's innocent delivery makes it more impactful. Well-crafted cringe.

jokes.text: I just found out, through some pretty impressive investigating, that your wedding was a sham. It's a green card marriage. That's crazy. I was born in south Carolina. These colors don't run, baby. Yes, but Wendy was born in Ottawa... Canada!
jokes.setup: Ron accusing Tom of fake marriage
jokes.punchline: Tom's patriotic defense, then revelation about Wendy being Canadian
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Tom's patriotic response before the Canada reveal. The 'Canada!' exclamation is funny.

jokes.text: I'll have your wife tonight. What? I'm just kidding. Get out of here. Why would you say that?
jokes.setup: Ron winning the dirt game
jokes.punchline: Makes inappropriate joke about Tom's wife, immediately backtracks
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ron's awkward attempt at inappropriate humor. Tom's reaction is perfect.

jokes.text: Do you want to watch a porno after this with me and my wife? No, Dave, 'cause you're disgusting. So it's definitely a no?
jokes.setup: Ann playing terrible date, making inappropriate suggestion
jokes.punchline: Follows up Leslie's rejection by asking for confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ann's terrible date character. The follow-up question makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You coy bastard.
jokes.setup: Leslie finally standing up during practice date
jokes.punchline: Ann's pleased reaction to Leslie's progress
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Ann's support. 'Coy bastard' is a fun phrase but not particularly original.

jokes.text: There is a man named Duke Silver. He hangs out at a bar in eagleton. He's an old friend of Ron's. Maybe you should ask him.
jokes.setup: Tom asking for dirt on Ron
jokes.punchline: Justin provides mysterious lead about 'Duke Silver'
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Duke Silver reveal. The mysterious name and location create intrigue.

jokes.text: I'm gonna dig up so much dirt, there's gonna be worms all over the place. It sounded snappier in my head.
jokes.setup: Tom making dramatic threat about finding dirt
jokes.punchline: Immediately acknowledges his metaphor didn't work
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment with Tom acknowledging his failed attempt at being clever. Self-aware humor works.

jokes.text: Me power. Good. You're officially first date-proof.
jokes.setup: Leslie gaining confidence from practice
jokes.punchline: Declares 'Me power' instead of girl power
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie character moment showing her quirky confidence. 'Me power' is endearingly awkward.

jokes.text: Help, police! Help! Help!
jokes.setup: Leslie going to Dave's house drunk and confident
jokes.punchline: Scene cuts to her screaming for help
jokes.explanation: Good comedic juxtaposition between confidence and immediate disaster. The cut to screaming works well.

jokes.text: I'm awesome, and you're lucky to have me. And I think our first date tomorrow's gonna go awesome, off the charts. Amazing. Up top. All right, let's do this bitch! I'm not scared. Can I come in? 'Cause I walked here 'cause of the drinking.
jokes.setup: Leslie showing up drunk and overconfident
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire confident declarations ending with drunk logic
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Leslie's drunken overconfidence. The practical admission about walking drunk is funny.

jokes.text: Ladies, ladies, ladies, it's just about that time. It's with the jazziest pleasure that I bring out for you my man, Mr. Duke Silver.
jokes.setup: Tom investigating Duke Silver at jazz club
jokes.punchline: Cheesy jazz club announcer introducing mysterious Duke Silver
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Duke Silver reveal. The cheesy announcer style builds anticipation.

jokes.text: As always, it is a thrill to be here during this witching hour with you lovely ladies. Now relax and let the Duke Silver Trio take you on a little journey... to yourself.
jokes.setup: Revelation that Duke Silver is Ron's alter ego
jokes.punchline: Ron speaking in smooth jazz persona completely unlike himself
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant character reveal. Ron as smooth jazz performer is perfectly unexpected yet believable. The 'journey to yourself' line is perfectly cheesy.

jokes.text: I think that's a sweater-shirt combo.
jokes.setup: Leslie confused about her fused clothing
jokes.punchline: Dave's casual identification of the fashion disaster
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about fashion disaster. Dave's matter-of-fact response is nice but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Are you impressed that I know what it's called? I ought to give you a lift home. Good. Well, in London, they call the elevators lifts, you're gonna give me an elevator home?
jokes.setup: Leslie proud of remembering 'bathroom'
jokes.punchline: Misunderstands 'lift' offer, launches into British accent
jokes.explanation: Good callback to bathroom confusion. Leslie's random British tangent is in character but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: When I was 16, I had sex with a married woman. When I was in college, I smoked a decent amount of pot. Nothing insane.
jokes.setup: Dave confessing his past to balance scales
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly serious confessions matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dave trying to be honest. The casual delivery of serious confessions works.

jokes.text: One time, this guy rang my doorbell at midnight to brag about getting laid when he was 16, so I shot him. Good night.
jokes.setup: Ann responding to Dave's late-night confessions
jokes.punchline: Claims to have shot someone for similar behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor from Ann. The casual delivery of extreme violence as response to Dave's behavior is perfectly deadpan.

jokes.text: Smooth as Silver and Hi ho, Duke. And look for my new CD next month, Memories of Now.
jokes.setup: Duke Silver finishing his performance
jokes.punchline: Ron's jazz album titles in his smooth persona
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Duke Silver character. The album titles are appropriately cheesy jazz names.

jokes.text: Say, 'I bested you'. I bested you.
jokes.setup: Tom taking victory photo with Duke Silver
jokes.punchline: Gets Ron to admit defeat while in jazz persona
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the dirt-digging competition. Tom's victory through discovering Duke Silver works.

jokes.text: Last night was our first date, so that would make tonight our second.
jokes.setup: Leslie expecting to cancel after embarrassing night
jokes.punchline: Dave reframes disaster as successful first date
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection showing Dave's positive interpretation of Leslie's disaster. Sweet character moment.

jokes.text: Well, we went on our first date, and I didn't even know it, a.k.a. I nailed it. No fires, no ambulances. Just good old-fashioned showing up drunk at a guy's house late at night.
jokes.setup: Leslie reflecting on her 'successful' first date
jokes.punchline: Lists drunk house visit as good old-fashioned dating
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie logic turning disaster into success. The 'a.k.a. I nailed it' and traditional dating description work well.

jokes.text: Hello! I drove a riding lawn mower through a Nordstrom! There's video that I took! It's on the Internet. Nothing.
jokes.setup: Andy frustrated no one found dirt on him
jokes.punchline: Reveals increasingly specific and documented scandal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - escalating to absurd confession while being ignored. The specificity of lawn mower through Nordstrom is brilliantly random.

jokes.text: I got hit by a fire engine. You are so lucky. How?
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining his injury wasn't plastic surgery
jokes.punchline: Getting hit by emergency vehicle seen as luck
jokes.explanation: Good Jerry moment where his accident is envied. The ironic 'lucky' response to getting hit by fire engine works.
